# Extractor Receipt

## Summary

This source contains empirical evidence about how public Claude Code configuration files are collected, structured, classified, and patterned across 328 files, including frequency counts for common concerns, limited use of examples/links/diagrams, and stated study limitations.

## Evidence Files

### file: evidence-01.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-04-029
evidence_id: evidence-01
kind: method
citation: Santos et al., "Decoding the Configuration of AI Coding Agents"
source_url: https://arxiv.org/abs/2511.09268
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: none
contradiction_relevance: none
---
The study collected and analyzed 328 Claude Code configuration files from public projects.

## Source Excerpt

The abstract states: "We collected and analyzed 328 configuration files from public Claude Code projects."

## Note

The source frames these as public Claude Code project configuration files, not a full census of all Claude Code usage.
```

### file: evidence-02.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-04-029
evidence_id: evidence-02
kind: method
citation: Santos et al., "Decoding the Configuration of AI Coding Agents"
source_url: https://arxiv.org/abs/2511.09268
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: none
contradiction_relevance: none
---
The initial GitHub Search API query found 4,724 repositories containing Claude.md.

## Source Excerpt

In "Repositories Detection," the paper says: "This initial search returned 4,724 repositories."

## Note

This count comes from searches performed between August 28, 2025 and August 30, 2025.
```

### file: evidence-03.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-04-029
evidence_id: evidence-03
kind: method
citation: Santos et al., "Decoding the Configuration of AI Coding Agents"
source_url: https://arxiv.org/abs/2511.09268
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: none
contradiction_relevance: none
---
The repository selection filtered out projects with fewer than 100 stars, non-English repositories, and repositories that were not real-world applications.

## Source Excerpt

The paper lists three exclusion criteria: "(1) Popularity. We filtered out repositories with less than 100 stars ... (2) Language. We excluded non-English repositories ... (3) Actual Applications. We removed repositories that are not real-world applications, e.g., awesome lists, tutorials, or configuration examples."

## Note

The "actual applications" filter was performed manually from repository description and README.
```

### file: evidence-04.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-04-029
evidence_id: evidence-04
kind: metric
citation: Santos et al., "Decoding the Configuration of AI Coding Agents"
source_url: https://arxiv.org/abs/2511.09268
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: none
contradiction_relevance: none
---
The final repository sample consisted of the top 100 most popular repositories by stars.

## Source Excerpt

In "Repositories Selection," the authors write: "From the remaining ones, we selected the top-100 most popular repositories, i.e., those with the highest number of stars."

## Note

This is a popularity-weighted sample, not a random sample of Claude Code repositories.
```

### file: evidence-05.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-04-029
evidence_id: evidence-05
kind: metric
citation: Santos et al., "Decoding the Configuration of AI Coding Agents"
source_url: https://arxiv.org/abs/2511.09268
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: none
contradiction_relevance: none
---
The selected projects spanned 23 programming languages, with JavaScript/TypeScript in 35 projects, Python in 16, and Go in 9.

## Source Excerpt

The paper states: "Overall, the projects are implemented in 23 programming languages, with JavaScript/TypeScript (35 projects), Python (16 projects), and Go (9 projects) being the most common ones."

## Note

These counts describe the selected top-100 repositories, not the full initial search set.
```

### file: evidence-06.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-04-029
evidence_id: evidence-06
kind: metric
citation: Santos et al., "Decoding the Configuration of AI Coding Agents"
source_url: https://arxiv.org/abs/2511.09268
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: none
contradiction_relevance: none
---
The selected repositories had a median of 950 stars, 488 commits, and 58 months since first commit.

## Source Excerpt

The source says: "All projects are fairly popular (median of 950 stars) and active (median of 488 commits). They are also long-lived, with a median age of 58 months since their first commit."

## Note

These medians describe repository popularity, activity, and age in the selected sample.
```

### file: evidence-07.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-04-029
evidence_id: evidence-07
kind: method
citation: Santos et al., "Decoding the Configuration of AI Coding Agents"
source_url: https://arxiv.org/abs/2511.09268
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: none
contradiction_relevance: none
---
During file fetching, the authors found 45 Claude.md files functioning only as memory banks and retrieved the referenced markdown files instead.

## Source Excerpt

The source states: "During this process, we detected 45 files functioning as memory banks, i.e., files that only pointed to other markdown files within their respective projects. In such cases, we retrieved the contents of the referenced files instead."

## Note

This means the dataset includes substituted referenced content for those memory-bank cases.
```

### file: evidence-08.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-04-029
evidence_id: evidence-08
kind: method
citation: Santos et al., "Decoding the Configuration of AI Coding Agents"
source_url: https://arxiv.org/abs/2511.09268
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: none
contradiction_relevance: none
---
The analysis extracted 2,492 section titles from the configuration files for manual classification.

## Source Excerpt

After describing the heading-extraction procedure, the paper says: "After following the previous procedures, we extracted 2,492 section titles."

## Note

The unit of manual classification here is section titles rather than every individual line of the files.
```

### file: evidence-09.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-04-029
evidence_id: evidence-09
kind: metric
citation: Santos et al., "Decoding the Configuration of AI Coding Agents"
source_url: https://arxiv.org/abs/2511.09268
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: none
contradiction_relevance: none
---
Claude.md files in the dataset had a median of seven level-2 headings.

## Source Excerpt

Table 1 and the surrounding text report: "As we can see, the files have a median of seven level-2 headings."

## Note

The same table reports a range from 0 to 213 level-2 headings.
```

### file: evidence-10.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-04-029
evidence_id: evidence-10
kind: metric
citation: Santos et al., "Decoding the Configuration of AI Coding Agents"
source_url: https://arxiv.org/abs/2511.09268
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: bounded
contradiction_relevance: tension
---
Thirty-six files had no level-2 sections and instead expressed practices and rules directly under level-1 headings.

## Source Excerpt

The paper notes: "Interestingly, we found that 36 files have no level-2 sections at all. After a manual analysis, we concluded that these files describe practices and rules directly at level 1 (# tag). Thus, in such cases, we decided to analyze the content of level-1 headings instead."

## Note

This shows that the heading-based analysis needed an exception path because some files did not follow the expected level-2 structure.
```

### file: evidence-11.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-04-029
evidence_id: evidence-11
kind: result
citation: Santos et al., "Decoding the Configuration of AI Coding Agents"
source_url: https://arxiv.org/abs/2511.09268
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: none
contradiction_relevance: none
---
Architecture-related guidance appeared in 238 of 328 files, or 72.6% of the dataset.

## Source Excerpt

In RQ1 results, the authors write that concerns and practices have "particular emphasis on specifying the architecture the agent should follow" and report architecture as "found in 238 Claude.md files from our dataset, 72.6%."

## Note

This is the most frequently reported concern among the listed categories.
```

### file: evidence-12.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-04-029
evidence_id: evidence-12
kind: result
citation: Santos et al., "Decoding the Configuration of AI Coding Agents"
source_url: https://arxiv.org/abs/2511.09268
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: none
contradiction_relevance: none
---
General development guidelines appeared in 147 files (44.8%), project overview in 128 files (39.0%), testing guidelines in 116 files (35.4%), and commands in 109 files (33.2%).

## Source Excerpt

Figure 2 discussion reports: "general development guidelines (147 Claude.md files, 44.8%), project overview (128 Claude.md files, 39%), and testing guidelines (116 Claude.md files, 35.4%) and commands (109 Claude.md files, 33.2%)."

## Note

These percentages are category frequencies within the analyzed 328-file dataset.
```

### file: evidence-13.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-04-029
evidence_id: evidence-13
kind: result
citation: Santos et al., "Decoding the Configuration of AI Coding Agents"
source_url: https://arxiv.org/abs/2511.09268
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: none
contradiction_relevance: none
---
Code examples were most common in Development Guidelines sections, appearing in 17.68% of instances classified in that category.

## Source Excerpt

In RQ2, the paper states: "the highest percentage of code examples appears in the Development Guidelines category (17.68% of the instances classified in this category)."

## Note

The source reports category-level percentages, not a single overall percentage across all files.
```

### file: evidence-14.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-04-029
evidence_id: evidence-14
kind: result
citation: Santos et al., "Decoding the Configuration of AI Coding Agents"
source_url: https://arxiv.org/abs/2511.09268
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: bounded
contradiction_relevance: tension
---
Links were rare, peaking at 1.83% in Architecture sections, and the authors found only two Mermaid UML diagram instances in the analyzed files.

## Source Excerpt

The paper says: "Conversely, links were most frequently found in the Architecture category, though in only 1.83% of the files." It then adds: "Finally, we also searched for diagrams in the files—specifically UML diagrams written in the syntax of the Mermaid tool, but found only two instances."

## Note

This evidence indicates that richer structured artifacts such as links and diagrams were uncommon in the observed configuration files.
```

### file: evidence-15.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-04-029
evidence_id: evidence-15
kind: result
citation: Santos et al., "Decoding the Configuration of AI Coding Agents"
source_url: https://arxiv.org/abs/2511.09268
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: none
contradiction_relevance: none
---
The most common configuration pattern was Architecture, Dependencies, and Project Overview, appearing in 21.6% of files.

## Source Excerpt

Table 3 reports: "The most common one includes practices on Architecture, Dependencies, and Project Overview, appearing in 21.6% of the files."

## Note

This is the top-ranked frequent itemset reported by the FP-Max analysis.
```

### file: evidence-16.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-04-029
evidence_id: evidence-16
kind: result
citation: Santos et al., "Decoding the Configuration of AI Coding Agents"
source_url: https://arxiv.org/abs/2511.09268
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: none
contradiction_relevance: none
---
Architecture appeared in all top-5 frequent Claude.md patterns reported by the study.

## Source Excerpt

The RQ3 summary states: "Architecture stands out as being the unique practice described in all top-5 patterns."

## Note

This evidence concerns the top-5 patterns listed in Table 3, not all possible patterns in the dataset.
```

### file: evidence-17.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-04-029
evidence_id: evidence-17
kind: claim-support
citation: Santos et al., "Decoding the Configuration of AI Coding Agents"
source_url: https://arxiv.org/abs/2511.09268
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: direct
contradiction_relevance: none
---
Claude Code reads the Claude.md file for each request to obtain project-specific guidance.

## Source Excerpt

Section 2 states: "For each request, Claude first reads the content of this file looking for information that can help it at fulfilling the request."

## Note

This describes the tool behavior the paper attributes to Claude Code.
```

### file: evidence-18.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-04-029
evidence_id: evidence-18
kind: incident
citation: Santos et al., "Decoding the Configuration of AI Coding Agents"
source_url: https://arxiv.org/abs/2511.09268
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: bounded
contradiction_relevance: tension
---
A stated threat to validity is that one author performed the classification, with review and confirmation by two additional authors.

## Source Excerpt

Section 7 says: "First, the classification of concerns and practices specified in the Claude.md files was performed by only one author, although it was reviewed and confirmed by two additional authors."

## Note

This caveat attaches to the manual taxonomy and categorization results reported in the paper.
---
